Question: A copper wire has a radius of $$200 \text{mum}$$ and a length of 5.0 m. The wire is placed under a tension of 3000 N and the wire stretches by a small amount. The wire is plucked and a pulse travels down the wire. What is the propagation speed of the pulse? (Assume the temperature does not change: $$( \rho = 8.96 \frac{\text{g}}{\text{cm}^{3}} , Y = 1.1 \times 10^{11} \frac{\text{N}}{\text{m}} ) . )$$

A pulse moving along the x axis can be modeled as the wave function $$y ( x , t ) = 4.00 \text{m} e^{\text{unicode[:-]} ( \frac{x + ( 2.00 \text{m/s} ) t}{1.00 \text{m}} )^{2}} .$$ (a)What are the direction and propagation speed of the pulse? (b) How far has the wave moved in 3.00 s? (c) Plot the pulse using a spreadsheet at time $$t = 0.00 \text{s}$$ and $$t = 3.00 \text{s}$$ to verify your answer in part (b).
Answer: a. Moves in the negative x direction at a propagation speed of $$v = 2.00 \text{m/s}$$ . b. $$\text{Delta} x = -6.00 \text{m;}$$ c.

Question: A string with a linear mass density of $$\mu = 0.0085 \text{kg/m}$$ is fixed at both ends. A 5.0-kg mass is hung from the string, as shown below. If a pulse is sent along section A , what is the wave speed in section A and the wave speed in section B ?

Consider two wave functions $$y_{1} ( x , t ) = A \text{sin} ( k x - \upomega t )$$ and $$y_{2} ( x , t ) = A \text{sin} ( k x + \upomega t + \upphi )$$ . What is the wave function resulting from the interference of the two wave? ( Hint: $$\text{sin} ( \alpha \pm \beta ) = \text{sin} \alpha \text{cos} \beta \pm \text{cos} \alpha \text{sin} \beta$$ and $$\upphi = \frac{\upphi}{2} + \frac{\upphi}{2}$$ .)
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{} \\  \\ \text{sin} ( k x - \upomega t ) = \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{cos} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) - \text{cos} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{sin} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \\ \text{sin} ( k x - \upomega t + \upphi ) = \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{cos} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) + \text{cos} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{sin} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \\ \text{sin} ( k x - \upomega t ) + \text{sin} ( k x + \upomega t + \upphi ) = 2 \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{cos} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \\ y_{R} = 2 A \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{cos} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} )\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: The wave function that models a standing wave is given as $$y_{R} ( x , t ) = 6.00 \text{cm} \text{sin} ( 3.00 \text{m}^{-1} x + 1.20 \text{rad} )$$ $$\text{cos} ( 6.00 \text{s}^{-1} t + 1.20 \text{rad} )$$ . What are two wave functions that interfere to form this wave function? Plot the two wave functions and the sum of the sum of the two wave functions at $$t = 1.00 \text{s}$$ to verify your answer.

Consider two wave functions $$y_{1} ( x , t ) = A \text{sin} ( k x - \upomega t )$$ and $$y_{2} ( x , t ) = A \text{sin} ( k x + \upomega t + \upphi )$$ . The resultant wave form when you add the two functions is $$y_{R} = 2 A \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) \text{cos} ( \upomega t + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) .$$ Consider the case where $$A = 0.03 \text{m}^{-1} ,$$ $$k = 1.26 \text{m}^{-1} ,$$ $$\upomega = \pi \text{s}^{-1}$$ , and $$\upphi = \frac{\pi}{10}$$ . (a) Where are the first three nodes of the standing wave function starting at zero and moving in the positive x direction? (b) Using a spreadsheet, plot the two wave functions and the resulting function at time $$t = 1.00 \text{s}$$ to verify your answer.
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{} \\  \\ \text{sin} ( k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} ) = 0 , k x + \frac{\upphi}{2} = 0 , \pi , 2 \pi , 1.26 \text{m}^{-1} x + \frac{\pi}{20} = \pi , 2 \pi , 3 \pi \\ x = 2.37 \text{m} , 4.86 \text{m} , 7.35 \text{m}\end{matrix}\right .$$ ;
